nameMeanings.name: Von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Ahaus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Sigismund, Arno, Erhard, Ewald, Franz, Hermann, Nikolaus.

German: topographic and occupational name for someone who lived and worked in a great house, from Middle High German, Middle Low German hūs ‘house’ (see Hausmann , and compare English House ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Haus ‘house’, one of surnames randomly assigned by Austrian clerks.

variants.name: Heusel
meanings: variant of Häusel, a diminutive of Haus , a nickname for someone in a small house.

variants.name: Hausmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Gunther, Arno, Benno, Berthold, Dietmar, Hans, Helmut, Jutta, Kurt, Lieselotte, Monika.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German hūs ‘house’ + man ‘man’, German Haus + Mann. In the Middle Ages the majority of the population lived in cottages or huts rather than houses, and in most cases this name probably indicates the steward of a great house or someone who had some other connection with the largest and most important building in a settlement. In some cases it may indicate a householder, steward at a castle, or the watchman on a tower. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Haushalter .

variants.name: Hauss
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Pierre.
German: variant of   Haus  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

nameMeanings.name: Johann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: When Johann I Von Ahaus was born in 1140, in Ahaus, Westphalia, Prussia, Germany, his father, Liefhard von Ahaus, was 36 and his mother, Kunigunde von Versfleth, was 15. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1221, in his hometown, at the age of 81.

Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1221, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 81 ans.

ko.Ahaus, Westfalen, Preußen, Duitsland: 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 웨스트파렌 크레이스 아하스
ko.lifeSummary: Johann I Von Ahaus(이)가 1140년 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 웨스트파렌 크레이스 아하스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Liefhard von Ahaus(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Kunigunde von Versfleth(은)는 15세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1221년 그의 고향에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.
